title: Love Personality Test
subtitle: Discover how you love, and how two loves interact

solo.description: One person answers questions and receives their love personality type

duet.description: Two people each answer questions separately, then get a combined compatibility report
duet.report_note: Each person gets their own love type first, then a combined compatibility analysis is shown

pole_a.name: Avoidant
pole_a.description: Uncomfortable with too much closeness, values independence in love

pole_b.name: Pursuer
pole_b.description: Leans into closeness, seeks reassurance and deep bonding

pole_a.name: Lover
pole_a.description: Leads with emotion, vulnerability, and romantic expression

pole_b.name: Guardian
pole_b.description: Leads with protection, stability, and practical devotion

pole_a.name: Depth-Seeker
pole_a.description: Needs profound emotional intimacy and full transparency

pole_b.name: Wonder-Keeper
pole_b.description: Preserves mystery and individuality even within love

questions.text: I feel most loved when someone gives me space to be independent.
I worry when my partner does not respond to messages for a long time.
I need alone time to feel like myself even in a relationship.
Physical and emotional closeness makes me feel secure, not smothered.
I express love through words, vulnerability, and emotional openness.
I show love by taking care of practical things so my partner does not have to.
I fall in love with how someone makes me feel, not just what they do.
I feel most loving when I can protect someone from pain or stress.
I want my partner to know every part of me, even the ugly parts.
I believe keeping some mystery alive is healthy in a long relationship.
I feel most connected when we have talked about our deepest fears together.
I value having my own inner world that even my partner does not fully access.
I sometimes feel like relationships ask too much of me emotionally.
I remember small details about my partner and use them to make them feel seen.
I am not fully satisfied in a relationship unless we have discussed our pasts completely.

types.name: The Romantic Lone Wolf
types.tagline: Loves deeply from a careful distance
types.description: You feel love intensely but you need space to process it on your own terms. You are not cold — you are selective. When you let someone in it means something real. But you have also used independence as a shield more than once, and the people who loved you most paid the price for it.
strengths: Emotionally authentic when you do open up
Never loses themselves in a relationship
Deeply loyal once committed
Low drama, high trust
challenges: You can emotionally starve a loving partner without ever realizing it
You often choose emotionally unavailable people because they feel safe — then wonder why nothing grows
When things get real, you create distance and call it needing space
You may have already let the right person walk away because closeness felt like losing yourself
types.love_style: You love quietly and from a slight remove. Your actions say more than your words, but your words rarely come. You are devoted in practice and distant in expression — and that gap is where most of your relationships break.
needs_in_partner: Someone emotionally secure enough to not spiral when you go quiet
A partner who has their own full life and does not need you to complete it
Consistency without surveillance — they show up but do not hover
Someone who reads actions as love without needing it verbalized constantly
red_flags: Someone who texts you asking if you are mad after six hours of silence — this pattern does not improve with reassurance, it escalates with it
A partner who uses your need for space as proof you do not love them — that logic gets weaponized every single time you need to breathe
Anyone who says they just need to know you are there and means it every hour — no amount of love you give will feel like enough to this person
Someone who punishes distance with guilt trips, cold shoulders, or ultimatums — that is not love, that is control dressed up as insecurity
A person who mistakes your emotional independence for emotional unavailability and tries to fix it — they will spend years fixing someone who was never broken
types.growth_edge: The version of you that is still using independence as armor has cost you more than you admit. Letting someone stay close is not losing yourself — it is the only way to find out if the relationship is real.

ALW.summary: Two people who have their own worlds and respect each other enough not to invade them. This is the rare pairing where both people feel free and still feel loved.

AGD.summary: The Protector is stable enough that you never feel smothered, and persistent enough that you slowly open. This works — but the Protector needs more depth than you naturally give.

AGW.summary: Neither of you needs the other to perform love loudly. Peaceful and real. Falls apart when a crisis hits and neither of you will say the hard thing first.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher will spend the entire relationship feeling rejected by your silences. You will spend it feeling suffocated by their need. This is the slow bleed relationship — neither person is wrong, but both people lose.

PLW.summary: The lightness works at first. But eventually the Pursuer wants more closeness than you can consistently give, and they start interpreting your distance as disinterest.

PGD.summary: The Devoted Guardian needs full emotional access and transparency. You will never provide that. This pairing breeds years of quiet resentment on their side and quiet guilt on yours.

PGW.summary: The Tender Keeper is gentle enough not to overwhelm you. This can work if they accept that your version of closeness will always have a ceiling they cannot break through.

types.name: The Free Romantic
types.tagline: Love as poetry, mystery as oxygen
types.description: You love with genuine feeling but you keep something permanently yours. You are not withholding — you believe that mystery is what keeps love alive. The problem is that the people who fall hardest for you eventually need access that you will never give, and you have spent more time saying goodbye than you have staying.
strengths: Makes love feel alive and constantly surprising
Preserves your own identity beautifully within a relationship
Emotionally warm without being consuming
Never loses the thread of who you are
challenges: You are often more in love with the idea of a person than the actual person in front of you
The mystery you preserve can become a permanent wall your partner never gets past
You tend to exit when real depth is required instead of leaning in
You may have romanticized keeping things unresolved because resolution felt like an ending
types.love_style: You pursue warmly and retreat gracefully. Love is an experience for you — felt deeply, shared selectively. You make people feel special in ways they struggle to explain, and you leave in ways they struggle to get over.
needs_in_partner: Someone who finds not fully knowing you romantic rather than maddening
A partner secure enough in themselves that they do not need to decode you
Someone with their own rich inner world who is not waiting for you to fill theirs
A person who understands that your privacy is not secrecy — it is self-preservation
red_flags: Anyone who calls your emotional privacy a defense mechanism that needs to be dismantled — they will spend the relationship trying to fix someone who does not want to be fixed, and resenting you for it
A partner who equates openness with love and sees anything kept private as a lie — you will never win this argument and they will never stop making it
Someone who pushes relentlessly for emotional access and reframes it as helping you — that is not help, that is pressure with a kind name
People who say things like I just want to understand you completely — run. That desire is about their anxiety, not your wellbeing.
A person who cannot be happy unless they know everything — you will either perform transparency you do not feel, or leave. Neither ends well.
types.growth_edge: There is a version of intimacy that does not cost you your mystery. You have not found it yet because you have left before it could exist. The unpolished, uncertain parts of yourself are not shameful — hiding them indefinitely is.

ALD.summary: Two people who honor each other's need for mystery without making it personal. This is quiet, real love with no performance required.

AGD.summary: The Protector is fascinated by the mystery at first. Then frustrated by it. Then quietly grieving the access they will never get. A slow fade if the depth conversation never happens.

AGW.summary: Two private people, both avoidant of oversharing. Peaceful and respectful. At risk of never going deep enough to weather a real storm.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher will push for total intimacy every single day. You will retreat every single day. This relationship ends — the only variable is how much damage happens first.

PLW.summary: Both warm, both wonder-keeping, both emotionally expressive without being consuming. This pairing works until one of you needs more than the other can give.

PGD.summary: The Devoted Guardian will feel perpetually shut out. They need full access and you will never provide it. This creates a specific kind of relationship grief that does not resolve.

PGW.summary: The Tender Keeper is gentle enough to appreciate the mystery without forcing access. Works if PGW can accept that they will never fully know you.

types.name: The Steadfast Protector
types.tagline: Love as a shelter you build every day
types.description: You love through reliability, protection, and a need to know someone completely. You are not the grand gesture type. You are the I remembered you mentioned that months ago and I already fixed it type. The danger is that your depth-seeking can read as interrogation, your protection as control, and your steadiness as boring — and some people will take advantage of your care without ever matching it.
strengths: Unwavering reliability that most people only dream of finding
Creates profound emotional safety for a partner who allows it
Loves with the long haul in mind — not just the feeling
Remembers everything, shows up for everything
challenges: Your depth-seeking can turn into interrogation before you notice the shift
You can become subtly controlling under the guise of caring and protecting
You struggle to be vulnerable yourself while expecting full access from a partner
You have stayed in relationships long past the point of return because leaving felt like failure
types.love_style: You love by creating safety and demanding entry. You protect fiercely and want to be known completely. That combination is either the most profound love someone has ever felt — or the most suffocating, depending entirely on who receives it.
needs_in_partner: Someone who wants to be fully known and does not treat that as intrusive
A partner who communicates openly and welcomes the depth you offer
Someone stable enough that your steadiness feels like love, not boredom
A person who lets themselves be protected without pushing the protection away
red_flags: Someone emotionally unavailable who accepts your care without offering their inner world in return — that is emotional theft and you will rationalize it as a project for years
A person who calls your need for depth controlling — if you need to know someone and they refuse to be known, that is incompatibility, not a flaw in you
Anyone who gets bored by reliability and starts chasing novelty inside the relationship — that restlessness will find reasons to leave no matter how much you give
Partners who use your patience as a guarantee — they know you will stay so they never have to try
Someone who pulls away the moment you offer real depth — if vulnerability scares them at the start, it will terrify them later
types.growth_edge: You give extraordinary love but you have built a wall between giving it and receiving it. The question is not how much more you can protect someone — it is whether you can let someone protect you back.

ALD.summary: Your stability helps the Lone Wolf open slowly. Requires patience you have — but they may never reach the depth you need.

ALW.summary: The mystery draws you in. The permanent mystery eventually defeats you. You need depth, and the Free Romantic guards their inner world forever.

AGW.summary: Two avoidant people who both lead with protection. Stable but potentially emotionally flat. Neither will push for depth, which is what you actually need.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher needs exactly what you offer: safety, steadiness, and depth. You need someone who wants to be completely known. This is the best match either of you will find.

PLW.summary: The Pursuer's warmth softens your seriousness. Your stability gives them the ground they sometimes need. Works well if PLW eventually lets you go deeper.

PGD.summary: Two guardians who both want depth and protection. The question is who goes first. Both are avoidant in different ways. Can stall into a comfortable but emotionally unfulfilling standoff.

PGW.summary: The Tender Keeper's warmth balances your intensity. They bring lightness you need. You bring seriousness they respect. A genuinely complementary pairing.

types.name: The Silent Devotee
types.description: You are deeply devoted in a way the world does not always recognize as love. You show up consistently, remember quietly, and protect without announcement. The cost is that your partner spends years wondering where they stand, not because you are absent but because you will not say the words. Silence is not cruelty — but it has the same effect when someone needed to hear something and you never said it.
strengths: Consistent and grounding in a way that is genuinely rare
Respects a partner's independence without resentment
Non-possessive but completely loyal
Creates a peaceful, low-pressure relationship environment
challenges: Problems fester in your silence until they become unsolvable
Your partner spends years guessing whether they are loved enough — that uncertainty is a form of emotional abandonment
You withdraw instead of fight, which means conflicts never resolve
You have let relationships end that you could have saved simply by saying something
types.love_style: You love through presence and action, never performance. You are there before someone asks and you stay without needing credit for it. But you have also watched people walk away confused about why you never just said it.
needs_in_partner: Someone who reads actions as love without needing constant verbal confirmation
A partner secure enough that your quiet does not read as indifference
Someone with enough self-assurance to not spiral during your silences
A person who does not perform their feelings and does not need you to perform yours
red_flags: Anyone who needs daily verbal affirmation to feel secure — your silence will become their evidence that something is wrong, and no amount of showing up fixes the anxiety you are not willing to talk through
A partner who interprets your quiet as coldness and escalates emotionally to get a reaction — they are looking for drama and your silence will fuel it

Someone who cannot handle uncertainty in a relationship — your emotional privacy will be permanent uncertainty for them and they will eventually either leave or make the relationship a constant negotiation
People who say I never know what you are thinking and mean it as an ultimatum — if that is where it starts, it never ends
A partner who needs you to process out loud regularly as a sign of emotional investment — you will fake it or withdraw and either way the relationship suffers
types.growth_edge: Being known is not losing your privacy. The people you love most have needed to hear things you have never said. The silence that feels protective to you has felt like abandonment to them. Saying one hard thing at the right moment would have changed everything.

ALD.summary: Both avoidant, both non-verbal about love. No pressure, no performance. Collapses in a real crisis when neither will say the hard thing first.

ALW.summary: Two private people who respect each other's inner world. Peaceful almost to a fault. The relationship may never go deep enough to be truly resilient.

AGD.summary: Two avoidant guardians. Stable and reliable but potentially emotionally flat. No one is pushing for depth here and the relationship can quietly drift.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher needs total emotional transparency. You will never provide it. They will feel emotionally starved for the entire relationship and blame themselves for it. Do not do this to them.

PLW.summary: The lightness works. Both of you keep the emotional register manageable. Risk is that neither pushes for the depth that would make the relationship last.

PGD.summary: The Devoted Guardian needs full access to your inner world. You guard it permanently. This becomes a specific, grinding resentment that quietly poisons the relationship.

PGW.summary: Both grounded, both warm in quiet ways. Works if PGW accepts that you will never initiate depth. Falls apart if PGW eventually needs more.

types.name: The Soul Searcher
types.tagline: Looking for someone to know completely
types.description: You love with a hunger for total intimacy. You want every layer, every wound, every dream. For you, love without depth is not love — it is company. The problem is that your intensity scares exactly the type of people you keep choosing, and you have spent more time chasing emotional access from unavailable people than you have actually being loved.
strengths: Creates levels of intimacy most relationships never reach
Completely devoted and emotionally fearless
Transforms both people in the relationship when it works
Never lets a relationship coast — always pushing for something real
challenges: Your pursuit of depth can feel like an emotional audit to someone not ready for it
You are unconsciously attracted to emotionally unavailable people — and you call it a challenge worth taking on
When you feel emotionally starved, you push harder, which drives the person further away
You have turned your own pain into a personality and called it depth
types.love_style: You dive. You ask the question no one else asks on the second date. You want someone to sit with you in the hard things and come back. When it works, it is the most profound love either person has felt. When it does not, it is devastating — for both of you.
needs_in_partner: Someone who finds total vulnerability exciting rather than threatening
A partner who actively shares their inner world without needing to be excavated
Someone who sees emotional intensity as the point of love, not a symptom of neediness
A person stable enough to hold space for your depth without flinching
red_flags: Emotionally unavailable people who find you interesting — they will not change, they never do, and you have already tried this and it cost you something real
Someone who deflects personal questions with humor, sarcasm, or a subject change — this is not charm, it is a preview of how every important conversation will go
A partner who uses the word deep as a negative — if they are threatened by your desire for emotional intimacy, this relationship will break you slowly
Anyone who says you are too much — that is not feedback, that is incompatibility, and the right person will never say it
People who are emotionally unavailable but expect loyalty and commitment in return — that is a one-sided contract and you will honor it far longer than you should
types.growth_edge: You do not have a depth problem. You have a selection problem. You have consistently chosen people who cannot give you what you need and spent years trying to earn access from them. The intensity you bring is not the issue — the people you have been bringing it to are.

ALD.summary: The Lone Wolf's distance will read as rejection every single day. You will push. They will retreat. This is a relationship where you do all the emotional labor and leave depleted.

ALW.summary: The Free Romantic will never give you total intimacy — ever. You will spend the entire relationship trying to break through a wall they have no intention of removing. This is the relationship that breaks you.

AGD.summary: The Steadfast Protector is stable, depth-seeking, and loyal. They will open slowly — but they will open. This is the relationship you have been looking for.

AGW.summary: The Silent Devotee shows love through action and never through words. You need words, depth, and transparency. You will feel emotionally starved the entire relationship and blame yourself for it.

PLW.summary: The Pursuer keeps things warmer but lighter than you need. You will push for depth they resist. They will charm you out of your frustration. The cycle repeats.

PGD.summary: Two people who want to be completely known. Profound when it works — can become codependent when it does not. The intensity is real and so is the risk.

PGW.summary: The Tender Keeper cannot match your intensity and will eventually feel exhausted by it. You will feel perpetually unsatisfied. Both of you deserve something closer to what you actually need.

types.name: The Enchanting Pursuer
types.description: You want closeness but you know how to keep things electric. You are warm, expressive, and genuinely good at making people feel wanted. You also use that warmth to stay at the surface when the deeper conversation arrives. You are the person who is great at relationships right up until they require something uncomfortable — and then you make it light again instead of going through it.
strengths: Makes people feel genuinely seen and wanted
Keeps the relationship feeling alive and new
Emotionally accessible without being consuming
Brings warmth and energy without demanding it back at the same level
challenges: You use charm to exit hard conversations before they finish
Your relationships stay in an extended early stage because you resist the shift into real depth
You are better at pursuing than staying — once the chase is over you get restless
You have called avoidance wonder-keeping and romance for long enough that you believe it yourself
types.love_style: You pursue openly and love warmly. You make the relationship feel like a constant discovery. The cost is that nothing ever fully resolves — the hard things get softened away, the depth never quite lands, and partners eventually realize they have been charmed but not truly known.
needs_in_partner: Someone who appreciates warmth and romantic energy without demanding total vulnerability in return
A partner emotionally available but not emotionally consuming
Someone who keeps their own identity alive within the relationship
A person who can receive love openly without treating it as an invitation to excavate
red_flags: Someone who needs to process every relationship moment at a deep emotional level regularly — your capacity for this is limited and their need is not, which creates a permanent imbalance
A partner who interprets your lightness as emotional immaturity and tries to fix it — they are looking for someone who does not exist inside you
Anyone who escalates the emotional register constantly in order to feel close — that urgency is not passion, it is anxiety, and it will exhaust both of you
People who confuse warmth with permission for total access — you can be warm and still have limits and the right person understands that distinction
A partner who needs the relationship to be serious all the time — seriousness is not depth, and pressure is not intimacy
types.growth_edge: There is a difference between keeping things light and keeping them shallow. You have been in that gray area for most of your relationships. Going deeper when the relationship actually calls for it is not losing the magic — it is whether the magic is real.

ALD.summary: The Lone Wolf appreciates your lightness. But eventually you want closeness they cannot give consistently. You will feel the ceiling.

ALW.summary: Two wonder-keepers who love warmly without making it heavy. Genuinely works. Risk is that neither of you ever pushes for the depth that makes a relationship last through hard things.

AGD.summary: Your warmth softens the Protector's intensity. Their stability grounds your restlessness. One of the better pairings for both of you when it works.

AGW.summary: Two people who keep the emotional register low. Peaceful. But no one is pushing for depth here and the relationship can stay pleasant while slowly hollowing out.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher will push for more depth than you naturally give. You will charm them out of their frustration temporarily. The cycle repeats until someone leaves tired.

PGD.summary: The Devoted Guardian wants full access. You keep things lighter than they need. You will feel pressured. They will feel shut out. Both of you will slowly stop trying.

PGW.summary: Both warm, both pursuers, different depths. Genuinely complementary. PGW brings gentleness, you bring energy. This pairing works well.

types.name: The Devoted Guardian
types.tagline: I will protect you and I need to know everything
types.description: You love by wrapping people in safety and needing full access to their world. You are not controlling — you are deeply invested. But the line between those two things is thinner than you think, and people you have loved will sometimes use the word controlling even when you believe you were just caring. The need to know everything, even when it comes from love, can feel like surveillance to someone who has not chosen that level of intimacy.
strengths: Creates profound security for a partner who wants to be fully held
Completely devoted — your commitment is not conditional
Notices everything and acts on it without being asked
Love is a verb for you — always active, never passive
challenges: The need for full emotional transparency IS a form of control, even when it comes from genuine love
You can become possessive without recognizing the shift
You have stayed in relationships where you gave everything and received very little and called it loyalty
When you feel emotionally shut out you become anxious in ways that push people further away
types.love_style: You love by pursuing completely and protecting fiercely. You want your partner's inner world and you offer yours without reservation. When this lands with the right person it is extraordinary. When it does not, it becomes a relationship built on one person's total investment and the other's polite tolerance.
needs_in_partner: Someone who genuinely wants to be fully known and finds that liberating rather than invasive
A partner who welcomes protection as love rather than reframing it as control
Someone who offers their inner world openly and does not make you extract it
A person who matches your emotional investment rather than accepting it passively
red_flags: Anyone who accepts your full devotion while keeping their inner world private — that imbalance will eventually become intolerable and they are banking on your loyalty to delay the conversation
A partner who reframes your protectiveness as controlling whenever it is convenient for them — they are using your love as leverage
Someone who is emotionally guarded but claims they want depth — people who want depth do not hide from it
Partners who need independence as a shield rather than a genuine preference — they are not avoidant, they are unavailable, and the distinction matters
Anyone who makes you feel like your investment is the problem — the problem is not that you love fully, it is that you chose someone who cannot meet it
types.growth_edge: You can love someone completely and still not need to know every thought they have. Learning to trust without requiring total transparency is not settling for less — it is the difference between intimacy and interrogation. The right person will give you access willingly. You should not have to extract it.

ALD.summary: The Lone Wolf will never give you full emotional access. You will feel perpetually shut out. This specific combination breeds a slow, grinding resentment that does not resolve.

ALW.summary: The Free Romantic guards their mystery permanently. You need full access. This is a relationship where you feel grief for the access you will never receive and they feel pressure they never signed up for.

AGD.summary: Two guardians who both want depth. Both avoidant. The question is who opens first. Can stall into a stable but emotionally hollow arrangement.

AGW.summary: The Silent Devotee will never give you the emotional transparency you need. Their silence will read as rejection to you. This quietly breaks down over time.

PLD.summary: Two depth-seekers who both want to be fully known. Can be extraordinary. Can also become codependent. The intensity is real and so is the potential.

PLW.summary: The Pursuer is warm but lighter than you need. You will want more depth. They will charm the edges off your frustration. Eventually both of you want something you are not getting.

PGW.summary: Both guardians and pursuers. Compatible energy, but PGW will not give you the depth you need. You will feel a ceiling you cannot break through.

types.name: The Tender Keeper
types.tagline: Close enough to hold you, wise enough to let you be
types.description: You are the rare type who can be warmly close without needing total access. You pursue with gentleness and protect without pressure. You make relationships feel safe and low-stakes in the best way. The cost is that hard conversations get avoided, problems accumulate under the surface peace, and the relationship can feel healthy right up until it is not — because you never said anything.
strengths: Creates emotional safety without suffocation
Pursues warmly without making it feel like pressure
Respects a partner's autonomy while staying genuinely close
Makes love feel effortless and uncomplicated
challenges: You avoid confrontation so effectively that real problems never get addressed
The peace you maintain is sometimes just avoidance with better branding
You can be in a failing relationship for months and still tell yourself it is fine
Your gentleness can be mistaken for passivity and people will take advantage of it
types.love_style: You love by creating warmth and holding space. You are there without being heavy. You protect without possessing. Partners feel genuinely comfortable with you in ways they struggle to describe. The shadow side is that your comfort-keeping means nothing uncomfortable ever gets fully resolved.
needs_in_partner: Someone who wants warmth and closeness without needing to be completely decoded
A partner who feels loved through presence and care rather than constant conversation about the relationship
Someone who appreciates gentleness and does not mistake it for emotional immaturity
A person who does not need you to perform emotional intensity to prove you care
red_flags: Someone who needs emotional intensity and urgency to feel loved — your gentleness will always feel like not enough to them, and that is not a problem you can solve
A partner who mistakes your emotional ease for shallowness and pushes you to prove your depth — the pushing itself is the problem
Anyone who creates drama to test your love — that behavior is a pattern, not a phase, and your patience will keep funding it
People who call your conflict-avoidance a character flaw while never examining why they need conflict to feel engaged — that dynamic will slowly make you feel broken for being calm
A partner who confuses peacefulness with the absence of feeling — you have deep feelings, you just do not weaponize them, and the right person understands the difference
types.growth_edge: The relationship that looks peaceful from the outside is sometimes dying quietly from the inside. You have let things go that deserved to be said. Initiating the hard conversation when it matters is not causing a problem — it is the only way to prevent a bigger one.

ALD.summary: Gentle enough not to overwhelm the Lone Wolf. But you will eventually want closeness they cannot consistently give. The ceiling is real.

ALW.summary: You appreciate the mystery without forcing access. Works if you can accept never fully knowing them. Many people cannot, eventually.

AGD.summary: Your warmth balances their intensity. Their depth challenges your tendency to stay light. One of the better pairings for both of you.

AGW.summary: Both grounded and non-dramatic. Works well. Also means neither of you pushes for depth, which leaves the relationship pleasant but possibly hollow over time.

PLD.summary: The Soul Searcher's intensity will exhaust you. You will feel like you are never enough for them. They will feel perpetually starved. Both people lose in this pairing.

PLW.summary: Both warm, both pursuers, genuinely complementary energy. PLW brings restless charm, you bring steadiness. Works well for both.

PGD.summary: The Devoted Guardian will want more depth than you naturally provide. You will feel their reaching as pressure. The gap does not close.
